subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: multilateral + independent (IMF, World Bank, UNICEF, UNEP, Freedom House, RSF, TI) + international press.
Southeast Asia's democratic and press-freedom standout (RSF's highest-ranked ASEAN state, peaceful transfers, ASEAN's 11th member in Oct 2025), facing a hard fiscal cliff.

Standout drags: near-total dependence on an unsustainable ~$18bn Petroleum Fund drawdown (possibly exhausted by the late 2030s, the salvation Greater Sunrise field stalled), full dollarization, and ~47% under-5 stunting despite oil wealth.

economic.notes: A dollarized economy on an unsustainable petroleum-fund drawdown with almost no non-oil base.

fiscal_state.notes: Near-total reliance on an unsustainable Petroleum Fund drawdown (>2x the sustainable rate) facing depletion by the late 2030s, with minimal non-oil revenue. (IMF 2025; World Bank)

monetary_independence.notes: Fully dollarized (uses the US dollar); zero domestic monetary policy. (IMF)

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: Uses the USD but issues no reserve currency of its own; note the ~$18bn (depleting) sovereign Petroleum Fund. (definitional; IMF)

inflation.notes: Very low (~1% in 2025), suppressed by dollarization. (IMF 2025; World Bank)

banking_stability.notes: A small, largely foreign-bank-dominated, shallow system with limited credit and capacity. (World Bank)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: Almost no non-oil exports (some coffee) - a tiny, undiversified, import-dependent economy. (World Bank; State Dept 2025)

institutional.notes: A functioning but fragile, capacity-deficient state with a strong-but-slipping press.

rule_of_law.notes: Functioning but fragile - CPI 2024 score 44 (a top-decade improver) yet courts face severe capacity deficits and due-process gaps. (TI CPI 2024; Freedom House 2025)

statistical_integrity.notes: Reasonable national accounts/CPI with donor support, but limited capacity. (INETL; World Bank)

civil_service_capacity.notes: Serious capacity deficits and oil-funded patronage employment undermining efficiency. (World Bank; Freedom House 2025)

press_freedom.notes: RSF 2025: 39th globally, ASEAN's highest and the region's only 'satisfactory' rating, though it fell 19 spots amid criminal-defamation cases. (RSF 2025)

political_social.notes: A free, personality-driven democracy with succession risk, over high poverty and youth unemployment.

political_stability.notes: A functioning democracy with peaceful transfers, but highly personality-driven (Gusmao/Ramos-Horta) with succession/generational-transition risk. (Freedom House 2025; Lowy)

civil_liberties.notes: Broadly respected - one of the freest environments in Southeast Asia, with isolated detentions. (Freedom House 2025; CIVICUS)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: Not a major migration destination; no large-scale mistreatment reported. (inference)

social_cohesion.notes: A largely cohesive post-conflict society, but youthful (two-thirds under 30) with high unemployment and a martial-arts-gang/faction-violence history. (Lowy; CIVICUS)

demographics.notes: Very young and fast-growing - a potential dividend but currently a jobs/services burden amid high poverty and stunting. (World Bank)

geopolitical.notes: A new ASEAN member with broad partners, conflict-free, with the Greater Sunrise dispute the main friction.

alliance_reliability.notes: A new ASEAN member (Oct 2025) with strong ties to Australia/Portugal/UN partners while engaging China - pragmatic and non-aligned. (Al Jazeera; Diplomat 2025)

conflict_involvement.notes: No active conflict; the main external friction is the (commercial/legal) Greater Sunrise pipeline dispute with Australia. (Stratsea)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: An open, dollarized, unsanctioned economy with free capital movement. (IMF)

physical_practical.notes: Weak healthcare with extreme child stunting, poor infrastructure, and natural-hazard exposure.

climate.notes: High vulnerability, very low readiness - rainfall variability, food insecurity, coastal/landslide on a poor half-island. (ND-GAIN; IPCC AR6)

healthcare.notes: A weak system - ~47% under-5 stunting, high child mortality and malnutrition despite oil wealth. (UNICEF; World Bank)

infrastructure.notes: Poor roads, electricity, and water outside Dili, with large oil-funded projects of contested value. (World Bank; State Dept 2025)

crime_safety.notes: Generally low-level crime but periodic political/gang unrest and weak policing capacity. (Freedom House 2025; CIVICUS)

disaster_insurance.notes: Negligible catastrophe-insurance penetration against meaningful natural-hazard exposure. (inference)

summary: Southeast Asia's democratic and press-freedom standout - RSF's highest-ranked ASEAN state, with peaceful transfers of power and a 2025 accession to ASEAN as its 11th member. But it sits on a hard fiscal cliff: the economy depends almost entirely on an unsustainable drawdown from a ~$18bn Petroleum Fund that the IMF warns could be exhausted by the late 2030s, while its salvation field (Greater Sunrise) stays stalled over the pipeline dispute with Australia and Bayu-Undan has stopped producing. Despite oil wealth, ~42% live in poverty and ~47% of young children are stunted, exposing a deep development failure and weak healthcare/infrastructure. The skew is negative - strong freedoms and political maturity cannot offset an oncoming revenue collapse, near-total resource dependence, and severe human-development deficits.
